description: E2E test case designs for opencode-agent crate - Agent orchestration and LLM integration

test_cases.title: BuildAgent.run() completes with tool execution
test_cases.description: Test BuildAgent.run() executes tools and returns response. Critical: test the Agent::run() method directly, not internal run_loop.
steps: Create Session with initial user message 'List files in /tmp'
Create mock Provider that returns tool_call for bash ls /tmp
Add tool result back to session
Call agent.run() again with tool result
Verify final response has no more tool_calls (user's turn)
expected_outcomes: First run returns AgentResponse with tool_calls
Tool calls parsed correctly with name and arguments
After tool execution and re-run, agent produces text response
edge_cases: Provider returns empty response (no tools, no text)
Provider errors - agent should return error

test_cases.title: PlanAgent enforces read-only constraints
test_cases.description: PlanAgent must return false for dangerous operations. Critical: UI relies on these flags.
steps: Call is_visible() - expect true (should appear in UI)
Verify all flags still match expected values
expected_outcomes: PlanAgent correctly reports can_execute_tools=false
PlanAgent correctly reports can_write_files=false
PlanAgent correctly reports can_run_commands=false
PlanAgent correctly reports is_visible=true
Model override does not affect permission flags

test_cases.title: PrimaryAgentTracker single-active invariant
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Only ONE primary agent can be active. This is a core invariant - two simultaneous activations must error.
steps: Verify state is Running and active_type is Some(Build)
Verify error contains both current=Build and attempted=Plan
Call begin_transition() - expect Ok(Build), state becomes Transitioning
deactivate() - expect Ok(Plan), state becomes Inactive
expected_outcomes: Single activation works
Second activation while active returns error with both types
Transition flow: Running -> Transitioning -> Running
Deactivation returns the deactivated agent type

test_cases.title: invoke_subagent creates isolated child with inherited permissions
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Subagent runs in isolation. Parent session must not be modified.
steps: Create AgentRuntime with BuildAgent and permission_scope=ReadOnly
Create MockExploreAgent that adds a message to session
Load parent session - verify still has only 2 messages (UNCHANGED)
expected_outcomes: Child session has separate ID
Permissions inherited correctly
Parent session is NOT modified by subagent execution
error_cases: Subagent execution failure propagates as SubagentError

test_cases.title: System agents hidden from UI listings
test_cases.description: CompactionAgent, TitleAgent, SummaryAgent must NOT appear in agent selection UI
steps: Filter all agents by is_visible() - should only get Build, Plan, General, etc.
expected_outcomes: System agents return false for is_visible()
User-facing agents return true for is_visible()
UI can safely filter with is_visible() == true

test_cases.title: Agent preferred_model affects provider selection
test_cases.description: When agent has preferred_model, runtime should select that model. Critical for cost optimization.
steps: Verify runtime passes correct model hint to provider
expected_outcomes: Model override preserved after agent construction
Runtime uses preferred model when calling provider.chat()

test_cases.title: AgentType serialization uses lowercase JSON
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: AgentType must serialize to lowercase for API compatibility
expected_outcomes: All 10 AgentType variants serialize to lowercase
Roundtrip preserves original value
Uppercase/mixed case JSON returns error

test_cases.title: TaskDelegate.delegate_task() full lifecycle
test_cases.description: Test TaskDelegate coordinates task delegation to subagent with proper status tracking
steps: Create Task via Task::new(description, agent_type, delegator_type, context)
expected_outcomes: Task starts Pending
Task transitions to InProgress during delegation
Task ends Completed with response
Task moved from active to completed

test_cases.title: AgentEvent emission during execution
test_cases.description: BroadcastEventEmitter must deliver events in order to all subscribers
steps: Verify subscriber receives all 4 events in order
expected_outcomes: All events delivered to subscriber
Event ordering preserved
Dropped subscriber receives no more events

test_cases.title: RuntimeConfig enforces iteration limits
test_cases.description: When max_iterations reached, runtime must stop and return error
steps: Mock provider that always returns tool_call
Call run_loop - expect error after 2 iterations
expected_outcomes: Runtime stops after max_iterations
Error correctly indicates limit was reached
edge_cases: max_tool_results_per_iteration limit - when too many tool results in single iteration

test_cases.title: Agent execution handles provider errors gracefully
test_cases.description: When LLM provider fails, error should propagate with context
steps: Create mock Provider that returns Err(LlmError::RateLimited)
Verify error is OpenCodeError::ProviderError or similar
expected_outcomes: Provider error propagated to caller
Error type correctly identifies cause

test_cases.title: Session lock prevents concurrent agent execution
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: When agent is running, session should be locked to prevent concurrent modifications
steps: Attempt second agent.run() on same session
Verify second call fails with SessionLocked error
expected_outcomes: Concurrent execution blocked
Session remains consistent
edge_cases: After first agent completes, session unlocked for next execution

test_cases.title: Agent handles parallel tool calls from single response
test_cases.description: When provider returns multiple tool_calls in one response, agent must handle all correctly
test_cases.risk_addressed: Provider returning >1 tool_calls (parallel tools) not handled correctly
test_cases.preconditions: ToolRegistry with ReadTool and BashTool registered
steps: Create session with user message asking to read multiple files
Mock provider returns 2 tool_calls in single response
Verify both tool calls are returned in AgentResponse
Execute both tools, add results to session
expected_outcomes: AgentResponse contains both tool_calls
Both tools executed in correct order
Results synthesized into single coherent response
post_checks: Final response references content from both files

test_cases.title: Nested agent call during tool execution
test_cases.description: When tool execution triggers another agent call, isolation is maintained
test_cases.risk_addressed: Tool execution modifying shared state that agent depends on
test_cases.preconditions: ToolRegistry with a tool that calls agent.run() internally
steps: Mock provider returns tool_call for a nested-triggering tool
During tool execution, nested agent call happens
Verify outer agent's session is not modified by nested call
Verify nested call has its own session
expected_outcomes: Nested agent call creates isolated session
Outer agent's session state unchanged after nested call
Tool result returned to outer agent correctly
post_checks: Outer session message count unchanged by nested call
Nested call has separate message history

test_cases.title: Agent visibility respects permission scopes
test_cases.description: Agent visibility may differ based on permission scope (ReadOnly vs ReadWrite)
steps: Create BuildAgent - is_visible() should be true regardless of scope
Verify system agents (CompactionAgent etc) return false in both scopes
expected_outcomes: BuildAgent visible in all permission scopes
System agents hidden in all permission scopes
Permission scope does not affect visibility classification

test_cases.title: PrimaryAgentTracker concurrent activation from multiple threads
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Verify single-active invariant holds under concurrent activation attempts. Race condition here could breach the core invariant.
test_cases.risk_addressed: Race condition breaking the single-primary-agent invariant
steps: Each thread calls activate() with random agent type (Build, Plan, Explore, etc.)
Verify exactly ONE call returned Ok, rest returned Err(MultiplePrimaryAgents)
expected_outcomes: Exactly one activation succeeded
All other activations failed with MultiplePrimaryAgents error
Error contains current and attempted agent types
State is consistent (Running with one active_type)
post_checks: Calling deactivate() returns the correct agent type

test_cases.title: Session lock released on agent panic
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Session lock must always be released when agent.run() panics. Permanent deadlock otherwise.
test_cases.risk_addressed: Session permanently locked after agent panic
test_cases.preconditions: AgentRuntime with session, mock provider configured
steps: Mock provider that panics on chat() call
Verify panic is caught and converted to Error
Immediately call agent.run() again on same session (without panic)
Verify second call succeeds (lock was released)
expected_outcomes: Panic caught and converted to Error result
Second call succeeds - lock was released
Session is not permanently locked
post_checks: Session can be used normally in subsequent calls
No zombie locks recorded in storage

test_cases.title: TaskDelegate cleanup on subagent panic
test_cases.description: TaskDelegate must not leak tasks into active state when subagent panics
test_cases.risk_addressed: Tasks stuck in active state forever after subagent panic
steps: Create subagent that panics during execution
Verify panic is caught by TaskDelegate
Verify task is moved to completed_tasks() or error is returned
expected_outcomes: Panic caught and converted to error result
Task not left in active state
Task appears in completed_tasks() with Failed status, or error returned
post_checks: TaskDelegate can accept new tasks after panic
No leaked state in TaskDelegate internal structures

critical_invariants: Only one primary agent active at a time
Tasks not leaked on subagent panic
high_risk_scenarios: agent_panic_001: Session lock deadlock on panic
agent_panic_002: Task leak on subagent panic
